channel.title: NYT > Business
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Stocks in Asia Rally and Oil Prices Drop
item.description: President Trump said the U.S. military would wrap up its campaign in Iran in two or three weeks.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Judge Blocks Trump’s Order to End Funding for NPR and PBS
item.description: The ruling will have minimal effect on the federal money going to public media because Congress voted to claw back funding. But it could have implications for any future funding.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin
item.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times

item.title: Allbirds, Once Silicon Valley’s Favorite Shoe, Sells for $39 Million
item.description: Despite once being valued at $4 billion, the company that made sneakers from Merino wool struggled to capture a wide customer base and turn a profit.
item.dc:creator: Kim Bhasin
item.media:credit: Jeenah Moon for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Allbirds store in Manhattan in 2018. The brand has closed all of its U.S. stores.

item.title: March Madness Puts Las Vegas Back in the Game
item.description: The influx of fans during this month’s college basketball tournament is a welcome lift to the city’s casinos and hotels after a dismal 2025, when the number of visitors fell 7.5 percent.
item.dc:creator: Ken Belson
item.media:credit: Roger Kisby for The New York Times
item.media:description: Crowds pack the Las Vegas sportsbooks to place bets on the N.C.A.A. March Madness basketball tournament and then watch who wins the games.

item.title: Global Ban on Digital Duties Expires After Stalled Talks at W.T.O.
item.description: A prohibition on taxing digital downloads expired, after members of the World Trade Organization concluded their annual meeting without an agreement.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jamieson Greer, the U.S. trade representative, said in a statement on Monday that he was “disappointed at the lack of seriousness” from other W.T.O. members.

item.title: Eli Lilly Will Buy a Narcolepsy Drug Developer for $6.3 Billion
item.description: Eli Lilly plans to acquire Centessa Pharmaceuticals, which has been conducting a midstage clinical trial of its lead drug.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins
item.media:description: The Indianapolis headquarters of Eli Lilly, which is flush with cash from sales of its weight-loss drug and has been on a deal-making spree.

item.title: McCormick to Combine With Unilever Food Unit to Create a Spice Giant
item.description: The deal ends Unilever’s time in the food industry and will create a multibillion-dollar group under the McCormick name.
item.dc:creator: Julie Creswell
item.media:description: An employee on the production line at a Unilever factory in Gloucester, England, last year.

item.title: FDA Is Expected to Lift Restriction on Peptides, Heeding RFK Jr.’s Wishes
item.description: The peptides, which are increasingly marketed as providing longevity and health benefits, were removed in 2023 from the agency’s list of products that compounding pharmacies can sell.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Dani Blum
item.media:credit: Hilary Swift for The New York Times
item.media:description: Some peptides, like insulin, have long been used as treatments, but many others have received little study and much remains unknown about their benefits or harms.

item.title: The Trump Effect at the Pump as Gas Hits $4 a Gallon
item.description: The average price of gasoline in the U.S. has topped $4 a gallon, a multiyear high, as war in the Middle East roils the oil market and slams drivers.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly and Brian O’Keefe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gas prices nationwide are continuing to rise, even as President Trump reportedly weighs a speedy end to the war in Iran.

item.title: Stocks Recover Sharply Amid Hope for Iran War’s End
item.description: Investors weighed the news that the owner of a Kuwaiti tanker loaded with oil said it had been hit in an Iranian attack near Dubai.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kuwait Petroleum Corporation/Handout, via Via Reuters

item.title: Whoop, a Wearable Health Device Maker, Raises $575 Million
item.description: With elite athletes like LeBron James and Cristiano Ronaldo as investors, the company, now valued at $10 billion, is courting everyday health enthusiasts.
item.dc:creator: Niko Gallogly
item.media:description: Whoop is one of several companies making wearable technology for wellness enthusiasts.

item.title: As Iran War Pushes Up Gas Prices, Fresh Food Distributors Add Fuel Surcharges
item.description: Delivering salmon, fruits and other perishable foods has become more expensive as the war with Iran pushes up diesel prices.
item.dc:creator: Julie Creswell
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Sandy Carson for The New York Times
item.media:description: As fuel costs rise because of the war in Iran, surcharges are being added to the shipping cost of certain food items, such as seafood imported from far-flung locations.

item.title: U.S. Gas Prices Hit $4 a Gallon on Average, a ‘Headache’ for Drivers and Trump
item.description: A month since the first U.S.-Israeli attacks and Iran’s response effectively shut off Persian Gulf oil, drivers are paying significantly more to fill up.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Lines in mid-March at a gas station in Hawthorne, Calif.

item.title: Employee Lawsuit Against Fox News Is Dismissed
item.description: A reporter who claimed the network had fired him for challenging its coverage failed to prove retaliation and discrimination, a federal judge said.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson
item.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
item.media:description: The U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021. Jason Donner, a 12-year employee, accused Fox News of firing him for raising concerns about its coverage of the Capitol riot and the 2020 presidential election.

item.title: T.S.A. Lines Appear to Be Getting Shorter as Workers Begin to Receive Paychecks
item.description: The hourslong waits appeared to be easing as Transportation Security Administration officers received their first pay in weeks. But with no funding deal, the lines could return.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung
item.media:description: Security lines at airports across the country generally shrank on Monday after President Trump signed an order to pay Transportation Security Administration officers on Friday.

item.title: Democrats Examine Elon Musk’s Role in Suspension of Business Disclosure Law
item.description: Lawmakers have asked the Treasury Department for information on how Mr. Musk may have influenced the decision to end enforcement of the Corporate Transparency Act.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Grind and Susanne Craig
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk was a top adviser to President Trump when the administration announced last year that it would stop enforcing the Corporate Transparency Act.

item.title: From Foe to Ally: The S.E.C. Is Now Writing Crypto-Friendly Policies
item.description: The embrace of crypto by financial regulators under President Trump has entered a new, cozier phase.
item.dc:creator: David Yaffe-Bellany
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times

item.title: Michael Rousseau, Air Canada’s CEO, to Step Down Amid Backlash Over Comments After Crash
item.description: Michael Rousseau’s mostly English statement after a fatal runway collision drew sharp criticism. The airline said it would emphasize French fluency in its search for a successor.
item.dc:creator: Kailyn Rhone
item.media:credit: Nick Lachance/Toronto Star, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Michael Rousseau, an Air Canada veteran, took over as chief executive of the airline in 2021.

item.title: Iran War’s Latest Economic Toll: Oil at $115 a Barrel
item.description: Economists and investors are increasingly pricing in a slowdown, or even a global recession, if the fighting drags on.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe, Lauren Hirsch and Christine Zhang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: More mixed messages from President Trump about war in the Middle East have markets and economists on edge.

item.title: Private-Credit Wobbles Could Prove Perilous for Trump
item.description: The Trump administration is poised to broaden access to risky investments that are showing signs of strain.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport and Colby Smith
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump’s executive order last year called for the inclusion of “alternative assets” in 401(k) plans, including private credit investments.

item.title: How Bard College Became a Major Real Estate Player 20 Miles Away
item.description: A nonprofit foundation donated $82 million worth of properties in Hudson, N.Y., to the school, which has provided few details about its plans.
item.dc:creator: Jim Zarroli and Cole Wilson For The New York Times
item.media:description: This 1818 building in Hudson, N.Y., the previous home of the public library, is among dozens of local properties that the nonprofit Galvan Initiatives Foundation donated to Bard College.

item.title: Asia Depends on L.N.G. From the Middle East. This Is What Happens When It Runs Out.
item.description: With a cutoff in shipments imminent, Asian countries, the biggest importers of liquefied natural gas from the Middle East, are already burning more coal and reducing consumption.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Residents waiting to receive canisters of household gas this month in Srinagar in Indian-controlled Kashmir.

item.title: Oil Wavers on Mixed Signals in Middle East Talks
item.description: Investors digested increasing signs of escalation of the U.S.-Israeli war in Iran, which has led to a blockage of critical energy supplies.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Politico Names Jonathan Greenberger as New Editor
item.description: Jonathan Greenberger, an executive at the publication, will take over from John Harris, a co-founder of Politico.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson
item.media:description: Jonathan Greenberger, who joined Politico in 2024, said in a statement, “My job is to push our journalism further and faster than ever before.”

item.title: New Political Group to Push Trump’s A.I. Agenda in Midterms
item.description: The group, Innovation Council Action, says it plans to spend at least $100 million. It will be led by a former administration official.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:description: Taylor Budowich, a longtime political adviser to President Trump, in the Oval Office last year. He will be leading the new Innovation Council.

item.title: ICE May Remain at Airports Even After T.S.A. Pay Resumes, Border Czar Says
item.description: Transportation safety officers are set to be paid on Monday, but Tom Homan, the White House’s border czar, said ICE agents may stay where there are shortages.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tom Homan contended on Sunday that wait times had decreased at airports since ICE agents arrived, doing identification checks and “plugging the security holes.”

item.title: How Many Air Traffic Controllers Are Needed Overnight?
item.description: The accident at LaGuardia has raised questions about whether the minimum standard of two air traffic controllers on overnight shifts is sufficient.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.media:description: The air traffic control tower at LaGuardia Airport in New York. LaGuardia operates under a noise-abatement curfew from midnight to 6 a.m., which discourages airlines from scheduling flights during that window.

item.title: Why the Iran War May Force Countries to Rely Less on Natural Gas
item.description: The U.S. and other exporters are poised for a windfall, but disruptions to Persian Gulf supplies are also pushing gas-buying countries to consider alternatives like coal, solar and nuclear energy.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Brad Plumer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: A liquefied natural gas plant in Cameron, La., last year. The United States, the world’s biggest gas exporter, will almost certainly benefit from shortages caused by the war in Iran.

item.title: He Was Supposed to Break Up Kraft and Heinz. He Changed His Mind.
item.description: Steve Cahillane, a food industry veteran, is trying to revive the company behind Kool-Aid, Oscar Mayer and many other brands.
item.dc:creator: Jordyn Holman
item.media:credit: Lyndon French for The New York Times
item.media:description: Steve Cahillane, a food industry veteran who took over at Kraft Heinz in January, previously led the split of Kellogg’s snacks and cereal businesses.

item.title: The Fragile Hope for Salmon Recovery in Maine
item.description: A long-term project to remove or modify dams may clear the way for endangered wild Atlantic salmon to swim freely up to the Sandy River. But it faces opposition from business and lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Murray Carpenter and Greta Rybus
item.media:credit: Greta Rybus for The New York Times

item.title: Chromebook Remorse: Tech Backlash at Schools Extends Beyond Phones
item.description: No more YouTube or video games on school laptops. Textbooks and pencils are back. Some seventh graders say they prefer learning offline.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Singer and David Robert Elliott

item.title: Check Your Target-Date Fund, Especially if You Plan to Retire Soon
item.description: Their simplicity makes the funds appealing, but they could leave many workers near retirement, particularly baby boomers, short on savings.
item.dc:creator: Brian J. O’Connor

item.title: The Air Canada Crash: Before and After the Frantic Call to ‘Stop, Stop, Stop’
item.description: How a cascade of seemingly minor events led to the worst runway disaster at LaGuardia Airport in decades.
item.dc:creator: John Leland, Pranav Baskar, Olivia Bensimon, Ashley Southall, Corey Kilgannon and Jan Ransom
item.category: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

item.title: 10 Million Grill Brushes Recalled After Some People Ingested Loose Bristles
item.description: In at least five cases, customers sought medical attention to remove bristles “from their digestive tract or throat,” a federal agency said.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy
item.media:credit: Colin Clark for The New York Times
item.media:description: Nexgrill said consumers should stop using the recalled brushes as the potential ingestion of bristles could lead to “serious internal injuries that could require surgery.”

item.title: The Biggest Names in Fine Dining Now Have Rock-Star Residencies
item.description: From Las Vegas to the Côte d’Azur, high-profile chefs are taking their cuisine on the road.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Kronsberg
item.media:description: For a residency of the Chicago restaurant Alinea at the Maybourne Beverly Hills last summer, remodeling the space alone cost “close to $400,000,” according to the restaurant’s top chef.

item.title: In an Asymmetrical War, Iran Seeks an Edge With Its Information War
item.description: Propaganda and disinformation have flooded the internet in a sophisticated effort to undermine support for U.S. and Israeli attacks.
item.dc:creator: Steven Lee Myers, Tiffany Hsu and Stuart A. Thompson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Valiasr Square in Tehran this month.

item.title: The Oil Shocks of the ’70s Changed the World. Will the Iran War Do the Same?
item.description: Gas lines and quadrupling oil prices freaked out American consumers. But the oil crises also transformed global finance.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Sommer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
item.media:credit: Pictorial Parade/Archive Photos, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Drivers line up for fuel during the Arab oil embargo in 1974.

item.title: Smaller Is Better in Silicon Valley’s ‘Tiny Team’ Moment
item.description: As artificial intelligence takes on more and more tasks, tech executives are embracing teams as small as two: one person plus A.I.
item.dc:creator: Lora Kelley

item.title: Can This Russian Bakery Survive a 3,500% Tax Increase?
item.description: The challenges faced by a small-business owner near Moscow highlight how Russia’s war-drained economy is on the doorstep of a major crisis.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Mashenka bakery outside Moscow last month.

item.title: Bank of America to Pay $72.5 Million to Settle Lawsuit by Epstein Victims
item.description: The suit claimed the bank had overlooked signs that Mr. Epstein’s accounts were being used to further his abuse of young women.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Goldstein
item.media:credit: John Taggart for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Bank of America spokesman said the bank “did not facilitate sex-trafficking crimes,” adding that “this resolution allows us to put this matter behind us and provides further closure for the plaintiffs.”

item.title: Trump Offers More Aid to Farmers, a Key Support Bloc Hurt by Tariffs and War
item.description: The president announced new loan guarantees at a White House event that appeared intended to reassure a group that has found itself caught in the crosshairs of some of his major policies.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: A tractor signed by government officials at the White House on Friday. Among other things, President Trump said he was urging major tractor companies to “produce a bigger, better tractor at substantially less money.”

item.title: Elon Musk Joins in Phone Call Between Donald Trump and Narendra Modi
item.description: Mr. Musk’s participation was notable because it is rare for a private citizen to be on a call between heads of state. It also suggests that Mr. Musk is back on better terms with the president.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong, Theodore Schleifer, Tyler Pager and Ryan Mac
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk and Shivon Zilis arriving for a wedding at Mar-a-Lago last month.

item.title: Iran Moves to Formalize Toll Plan in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tehran has effectively closed off the critical waterway, turning back container ships on Friday, and Iranian lawmakers are considering whether to formalize charging fees to pass.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran has effectively cut off traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, leaving thousands of vessels idling on either side of it.

item.title: Stocks Keep Falling as Investor Lose Patience With the War in Iran
item.description: The S&P 500 is down nearly 9 percent from a January high. The weekly losing streak is its worst in roughly four years.
item.dc:creator: Joe Rennison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Versant, Owner of MS NOW, in Talks to Acquire Vox Media’s Podcast Division
item.description: Versant, the parent company of CNBC and MS NOW, is looking to continue expanding its business beyond cable TV.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin and Jessica Testa
item.media:description: Mark Lazarus, center, the chief executive of Versant, at the New York Stock Exchange last year.

item.title: Global Food Supply Faces a Dangerous Bottleneck as Iran War Persists
item.description: Fertilizer prices are climbing as a result of disruptions in the Middle East, putting global food supplies at risk.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A farmer spread fertilizer on his mustard field this week in Pampore, India. Large parts of Asia, particularly India and Thailand, are most exposed to fertilizer supply shortages.

item.title: The Iran War Has Diesel Prices Soaring, and Consumers Could Pay the Price
item.description: Diesel has climbed faster than gasoline this month, directly hitting businesses, from brewers to trucking companies. Those higher costs are likely to make their way into consumer prices.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The rising price of diesel fuel is driving up costs for Maui Brewing, a brewery in Kihei, Hawaii, that imports raw materials to make its beer.

item.title: Many People Will Need Long-Term Care, but Most Don’t Have Insurance to Cover It
item.description: Care for people who can’t take care of themselves is expensive, and regular health insurance doesn’t cover the cost.
item.dc:creator: Ann Carrns

item.title: Savannah Guthrie Is Returning to ‘Today’
item.description: NBC said that she would return as an anchor of the show on April 6, a little more than two months after the disappearance of her mother.
item.dc:creator: John Koblin
item.media:description: Savannah Guthrie on the “Today” set at Rockefeller Plaza this month.

item.title: How the Iran War Is Costing the Economy Its Buffers
item.description: As the U.S.-Israeli campaign enters a second month, analysts see a growing toll. One forecast predicts oil hitting $200 a barrel.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe and Ian Mount
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: War in the Middle East has roiled markets and ignited fears of global economic damage.
